exemplar.title: Mathematical Modeling Water Spray Angles and Find Optimal Angle in Forest Fire Suppression

detailedExemplarData.title: Mathematical Modeling Water Spray Angles and Find Optimal Angle in Forest Fire Suppression

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear macroâstructure with table of contents and section headings
Logical narrative progression (introduction â model development â optimisation â evaluation)
Concise section headings (e.g. âOptimize the angleâ) aid navigation
weaknesses: Algebraic gaps and abrupt jumps interrupt logical flow
Mis-numbered figures and duplicated âConclusionâ entries reduce polish
Redundant narrative and overlong quotations detract from conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is coherent and generally well organised (band 3). There is a clear structure, labelled figures and sign-posted appendices, and most content is relevant to the investigation. However, mis-numbered figures, inconsistent footnote formatting, duplicated derivations and occasional irrelevant narrative prevent it from reaching the highest band (4). A score of 3 reflects strong organisation and logical flow with room for conciseness and polish.

strengths: Consistent use of standard mathematical symbols (Î¸, vâ, g) in most derivations
Integration of multiple representations (algebraic, graphical, photographic, Python plot)
Step-by-step derivations with explanatory purpose sentences
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (Cos vs cos), missing differentials and variable misuse (x vs t)
Graphs lack axis labels and units
Unjustified approximations (e^{âkx}â1) and unexplained algebraic jumps limit clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication is mostly clear, with appropriate notation and a range of representations used effectively (pushing into band 3). There remain lapses in notation consistency and unexplained steps that prevent a full band-4 performance. A score of 3 recognises generally good mathematical communication, tempered by these weaknesses.

strengths: Personal interest in Californian wildfires provides clear context and motivation
Proposal to include environmental variables and experimental validation shows high initiative
Gaussian efficiency factor ties studentâs own modelling choices to real-world concerns
weaknesses: Core derivations closely follow standard projectile motion, limiting originality
Limited data collection or alternative modelling beyond the literature
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is outstanding evidence of independent thinking and a highly personal and innovative approach in parts of the investigation (e.g. bespoke efficiency function and experimental proposals). Although the derivations mirror standard methods, the studentâs own model extensions and real-world tie-ins justify the top band (3) for personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on why the optimal angle differs from literature values
Thoughtful qualitative analysis of model assumptions and parameter sensitivity
weaknesses: Evaluation of outcomes is brief and lacks quantitative analysis
Reflection is descriptive in places and absent during derivations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present throughout with thoughtful analysis of results (band 2), but it remains qualitative and the evaluation of outcomes is limited (brief list of future improvements without systematic assessment). A score of 2 reflects meaningful but not substantial critical reflection.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (projectile motion, Gaussian efficiency, differentiation, numerical solution) used effectively to meet the aim
Integration techniques (u-substitution) and derivation of the range function are handled correctly
Use of course-level calculus and exponentials pushes beyond basic examples
weaknesses: Algebraic simplification errors (mixing x and t in decay term, omission of f(Î¸) in some integrals)
Python code in the appendix contains syntax errors and lacks reproducibility details
No justification for dismissing the trivial root T=0 or reporting specific numerical results in the conclusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is used comprehensively and beyond standard coursework examples, reflecting a strong grasp of SL-level methods (band 5). Minor errors in algebra and code prevent an expert-level (6) performance, but the sophisticated modelling and correct application of integration and optimisation justify a high mark of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: Paragraphs usually begin with a sentence explaining purpose, derivatives are set out step-by-step and conclusions are stated.  Yet several algebraic lines are duplicated, and the switch from general decay to the small-k approximation is not explained, limiting clarity.  Hence the communication is âsomewhatâ structured rather than consistently clear.

Derivations of time-of-flight and range are mostly correct, yet errors appear (e.g., mixing x and t in decay term, omission of f(Î¸) in some integrals, algebraic simplification mistakes).  These indicate good but not thorough understanding, warranting âgood understanding with minor errorsâ.

The student employs algebraic equations, one graph, a photograph of equipment and a Python plot.  These satisfy the expectation of âsomeâ multiple representations, but the plot does not incorporate the decay factors discussed, and no tables or diagrams illustrate parameter sensitivity, so effectiveness remains moderate.

The investigation follows a recognizable narrative: introduction, model development, optimisation, and evaluation. Ideas generally build on one another (derivation of f(Î¸) â parametric equations â range function â differentiation) so the reader can trace the argument.  Nevertheless, algebraic gaps, abrupt jumps (e.g., substitution of small-k â1 without justification) and some duplicated derivations interrupt the logical flow, preventing the work from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Introducing a Gaussian efficiency factor and writing a short Python script show some initiative, yet the mathematical creativity is limited; no alternative models, sensitivity studies or original data are attempted.  Hence only âsomeâ creativity is demonstrated.

The student analyses sources of discrepancy (parameter choice, simplifications, turbulence) and comments on model sensitivity, representing thoughtful analysis.  The discussion remains qualitative; no quantitative error analysis or alternative computations are offered, so insight is good but not âdeep and insightfulâ.

A brief list of future improvements (vary k, include terrain, validate experimentally) constitutes a limited evaluation of outcomes.  It lacks a systematic assessment of accuracy or implications for real firefighting practices, placing it in the âlimitedâ band.

The Evaluation section discusses why the modelâs 24.27Â° differs from literature and lists several simplifying assumptions, showing meaningful reflection integrated through the report.  However, reflection is descriptive rather than critical in places and is absent during derivations, so the depth is âmeaningfulâ but not âsubstantialâ.

Standard mathematical symbols (Î¸, vâ, g) and functions are used correctly in many places, but inconsistent capitalisation (Cos vs cos), missing differentials and the mistaken mix of x and t in vâ(t)=vâcosÎ¸ eâ»áµË£ detract from accuracy.  Overall, appropriate language is present âsome of the timeâ.

The exploration is clearly tied to the studentâs concern about Californian wildfires and applies mathematics to that context, indicating a genuine personal approach.  Still, the model closely follows an existing research outline and lacks novel data collection, keeping the evidence at the âclearâ rather than âhighly innovativeâ level.

Most material is relevant to modelling the spray angle, yet lengthy narrative about news articles, duplicated derivative steps and an overlong quotation of Python output add bulk without mathematical value.  These digressions and repetitions mean the piece is âmostlyâ rather than fully concise.

Projectile motion, Gaussian efficiency, differentiation and basic numerical solution are all relevant and used with a clear purpose: determining range and optimal angle.  Despite simplifications, the mathematics directly addresses the aim and is therefore âused effectivelyâ.

Choosing to question an academic articleâs 37Â° angle and constructing a personalised efficiency function shows meaningful independent thinking.  However, most derivations replicate standard projectile motion and the optimisation is performed with a GDC, so evidence is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

A table of contents, section headings and labelled figures give a clear macro-structure.  Sections appear in a sensible order and the appendix is sign-posted.  However, mis-numbered figures, repeated âConclusionâ headings and an appendix code snippet that does not compile show that the structure, while clear, is not polished to the âexceptionally well organisedâ standard.

The work sits squarely at SL level: trigonometry, exponentials, basic calculus, numerical graphing.  Inclusion of an air-resistance decay term pushes slightly beyond textbook examples but remains accessible.  Thus, the mathematics is âcommensurate with course levelâ but not fully comprehensive.

annotations.comment: The mention of the research paper on optimal spray angle is not accompanied by an in-text citation. Please add a proper reference here to maintain academic rigor.
The table of contents repeats the âConclusionâ entry and lacks page numbers. Removing duplicate entries and adding page references will improve navigation and overall coherence.
Including page numbers in the table of contents will allow the reader to locate sections more efficiently and strengthen the reportâs organization and structure.

The personal interest section effectively links the studentâs international perspective and concern for wildfires to the mathematical investigation, demonstrating clear evidence of personal engagement.
The aim statement is clearly articulated, but it reiterates âfire suppressionâ multiple times. Reducing redundancy will make this section more concise.
Figure 1 is well integrated, with a clear caption and source. This visual effectively supports the description of the fire-fighting equipment.
The final form x(t)=(vâ cosÂ²Î¸/k)(1âe^{âk x}) retains x in the exponent, conflicting with t as the integration variable. Clarify variable notation to avoid confusion.
The section heading âOptimize the angleâ clearly delineates the next phase of the investigation, strengthening the reportâs logical structure.
Solving y(T)=0 yields T=0 and the nonzero root. Briefly justify discarding the trivial T=0 solution to show full mathematical rigour.
The approximation e^{âk x}â1 for small k is reasonable, but the student should estimate kâs magnitude or show residual error to justify this simplification.
Figure 2 aptly visualizes the water-jet trajectory, but lacks axis labels and units. Adding these improves mathematical communication and reader understanding.
The evaluation outlines several model simplifications but doesnât quantify their impact. A brief parameter study would strengthen the analysis of outcomes.
The methodology outline logically sets out three steps. To enhance clarity, explicitly list any simplifying assumptions in this section.
The footnote numbering in the reference appears inconsistent with in-text superscripts. Ensure that each citation number matches its footnote to avoid confusion.
The plan to derive the range function R(Î¸) and assign realistic values to vâ, k, and a demonstrates effective use of relevant mathematics.

The detailed narrative on airflow decay spans multiple paragraphs without introducing new equations. Consider summarizing the conceptual points to improve conciseness and focus on mathematical content.
In D(Î¸)=e^{âk(Î¸âÎ¸â)Â² 2}, the exponent syntax is unclear. Clarify whether the factor â2â multiplies k or the square, and correct to standard form e^{âk(Î¸âÎ¸â)Â²}.
The expression vâ(t)=vâ cos Î¸Â·f(Î¸)Â·e^{âk x} mixes x as both independent variable and integration limit. Replace e^{âk x} with e^{âk t} or clarify the dependency.
When introducing the parametric equations vâ(t) and v_y(t), restating the definitions of all variables (especially f(Î¸)) will help maintain logical clarity for the reader.
The integral x(t)=â«vâ(t) dt should explicitly include the differential dt. Proper notation increases precision in mathematical communication.
The substitution u=e^{âkx} and change of variables for x(t) is handled correctly, demonstrating sound integration technique.
The derivation R(Î¸)=2vâÂ² cos Î¸ sin Î¸ fÂ²(Î¸)/g correctly applies the trigonometric identity. Well presented use of mathematics at SL level.
The step differentiating fÂ²(Î¸) via the product rule is correct. Label each substep clearly to enhance logical clarity in mathematical communication.
The footnote marker for the product rule appears as â<sup>4</sup>â but is mismatch with in-text style. Harmonize footnote formatting to maintain presentation standards.
Setting dR/dÎ¸=0 and solving numerically via a GDC is appropriate; including details of your initial estimate and step size would improve reproducibility.
The concluding paragraph on page 14 repeats narrative already stated. Condensing this discussion will increase conciseness and relevance.
The evaluation acknowledges parameter sensitivity but remains qualitative. Incorporating a simple sensitivity calculation for k or a would deepen critical reflection.
The conclusion mentions broad outcomes without stating specific numerical results. Including Î¸_opt â24.27Â° and Râ30.7 m would increase precision.
The Python code in the appendix contains syntax errors (âSumportâ, undefined v0, typo in linspace). Correcting these will ensure the script compiles and supports reproducibility.
The conclusion restates success but omits mention of limitations discussed earlier. Referencing these would demonstrate deeper critical analysis.
The discussion connects mathematical modelling to real-world fire suppression, highlighting the studentâs independent thinking and personal engagement.
The future work section on varying terrain and nozzle geometry is thorough. Prioritizing one scenario and outlining specific next steps would increase focus.
The proposal to include environmental variables and experimental validation reflects outstanding personal initiative and creative engagement.
The final conclusion recaps achievements but would benefit from referencing the initial personal interest to reinforce coherence.
